name: JFrog Artifactory (PyPI repositories)
license: Proprietary (commercial); free Cloud tier and limited OSS/CE editions exist (Artifactory OSS is Apache-2.0 but covers Maven/generic only, not PyPI)
cost: Cloud: free tier (2 GB storage / 10 GB monthly transfer); Pro $150/mo (currently $50/mo promo, 25 GB consumption); Enterprise X from $950/mo (125 GB, HA, SSO, federation); Enterprise+ custom. Self-managed: Pro X from ~$27,000/yr (1 server), Enterprise X from ~$51,000/yr (3 servers), Enterprise+ custom (6 servers). Consumption (storage+transfer) billed per-GB above included tiers; Xray/Advanced Security/Curation are paid add-ons.
language: Java (core); several JFrog Platform microservices in Go (Access, Router, Metadata)
repo: https://github.com/jfrog (charts, CLI, clients OSS; core product is closed-source)
perf_notes: Java/Tomcat app with checksum-based, deduplicated binary storage; throughput scales with the HA cluster node count and shared object store. No published per-PyPI-request benchmarks; on large clusters JFrog warns some admin/metrics API calls are slow enough to time out (Prometheus exporter offers --use-cache). Remote PyPI repos cache on first request and serve subsequent hits locally.
scale_notes: Enterprise HA: active/active multi-node cluster sharing a PostgreSQL database and a single object-store filestore (S3/GCS/Azure); nodes scale horizontally behind a load balancer. Federation/replication mirrors repos across geographically distributed Artifactory instances. Runs at very large enterprise scale (hundreds of TB, billions of artifacts) but is a stateful clustered system, not a stateless single binary.
summary: Enterprise universal binary repository manager whose PyPI repository type provides local (private), remote (on-demand proxy/cache), and virtual (merged) Python indexes with deep RBAC, SSO, and (via Xray/Curation add-ons) supply-chain security.
extras: Universal repo manager: 30+ package types (npm, Maven/Gradle, Docker/OCI, NuGet, Go, Cargo, Debian/RPM, Helm, Conda, Hugging Face, etc.) alongside PyPI
PyPI repositories support both PEP 503 HTML and an optional PEP 691 JSON Simple API ('Enable simple json format'); virtual repos serve JSON only if all sub-repos can, and fall back to HTML otherwise
PyPI Enforce Layout (>=7.70.2) validates filenames/paths against internal wheel/sdist metadata
Virtual repos merge local + remote PyPI with configurable resolution order; exclude patterns mitigate dependency-confusion leakage to upstream
Checksum-based, deduplicated filestore with signed-URL direct cloud download (enableSignedUrlRedirect)
JFrog Xray (paid): binary-level SCA, CVE + malware detection, license policy, CycloneDX/SPDX SBOM, download blocking/quarantine
JFrog Curation (paid): policy gate that blocks malicious/non-compliant packages at resolution time
JFrog Evidence + Release Bundles for signed provenance/attestation (Sigstore-style), distinct from PEP 740
OIDC token exchange via setup-jfrog-cli for keyless CI publishing (GitHub Actions, Azure DevOps); SCIM user provisioning. This is JFrog platform OIDC auth, not PyPI/Warehouse-style trusted publishing
REST API + JFrog CLI; Terraform provider for repo provisioning
Replication/Federation for multi-site repo sync; user plugins (Groovy) for custom cleanup/automation
